import pandas as pd

data = {'order_id': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 'order_date': ['01jan2022', '02jan2022', '03jan2022', '04jan2022', '05jan2022'], 'order_total': [100, 200, 300, 400, 500]}
orders = pd.DataFrame(data)
# Convert order_date to datetime
orders['order_date'] = pd.to_datetime(orders['order_date'], format='%d%b%Y')
# Calculate running total
orders['cumulative_total'] = orders['order_total'].cumsum()
# Calculate lag of order_total
orders['lag_order_total'] = orders['order_total'].shift(1)
print(orders)

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# define master dataset
orders = pd.DataFrame({
    'order_id': [1, 2, 3],
    'order_date': [20220101, 20220115, 20220201]
})

# define transactional dataset
order_items = pd.DataFrame({
    'order_id': [1, 1, 2, 2, 3],
    'item_id': [101, 102, 201, 202, 301],
    'item_name': ['itemA', 'itemB', 'itemC', 'itemD', 'itemE']
})

# perform outer join (zipper join equivalent)
merged = pd.merge(orders, order_items, on='order_id', how='outer', suffixes=('', '_match'))

# forward-fill to propagate order_id
merged['order_id'] = merged['order_id'].fillna(method='ffill')

# perform update logic
merged['update_order'] = np.where(merged['order_id'] == merged['order_id_match'], 1, 0)

# clean-up
merged = merged.drop(['order_id_match'], axis=1)

# print updated orders
print(merged[['order_id', 'order_date', 'update_order']])
